title: Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide
content:
{{Top|rwm|leg|ref|italics=1}} {{ReferenceBook |image=[[File:SWRogeuSquadron-TheOfficialNintendoPlayersGuide.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron:
The Official Nintendo Player's Guide'' |author=[[Levi Buchanan]], [[Jason Leung]], [[Scott Pelland]], [[Paul Shinoda]] {{C|Writers/Editors}}''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor=*[[Leslie Swan]] {{C|Editor in Chief}} *[[Jessica Joffe Stein|Jessica Joffe]] {{C|Copy Editor}} |publisher=[[Nintendo|Nintendo of America]] |release date=c. [[March]] [[1999]]''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' was advertised as being available in the final pages of {{BookCite|title=[[Nintendo Power]]|number=issue 118|publisher=[[Nintendo|Nintendo of America]]|date=[[March]] [[1999]]|link=https://archive.org/details/nintendo-power-issue-127-december-1999/Nintendo%20Power%20Issue%20118%20March%201999}}. |type=Paperback |pages=112 |dimensions= |weight= |isbn=0454066937 |series= |preceded by= |followed by=''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' }} '''''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide''''', also called '''''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'' Player's Guide''',''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' copyright section was a strategy guide for the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']] from [[LucasArts]] published by [[Nintendo|Nintendo of America]] for the ''[[Nintendo Power]]'' magazine in around [[March]] [[1999]]. It was written by [[Levi Buchanan]], [[Jason Leung]], [[Scott Pelland]] and [[Paul Shinoda]]. ==Contents== *Briefing **Rebels with a Cause **Basic Training **The Hangar ***X-wing Schematic *Chapter I: The Rebel Opposition **Stage 1: Ambush at Mos Eisley ***Sandcrawler Schematic **Stage 2: Rendezvous at Barkhesh ***TIE Interceptor Schematic **Stage 3: The Search for the ''Nonnah'' ***A-wing Schematic **Stage 4: Defection at Corellia ***''Millennium Falcon'' Schematic **Stage 5: Liberation of Gerrard V ***AT-PT Schematic *Chapter II: Rogue Squadron **Stage 6: The Jade Moon ***TIE Bomber Schematic **Stage 7: Imperial Construction Yards ***AT-AT Schematic **Stage 8: Assault on Kile II ***Y-wing Schematic **Stage 9: Rescue on Kessel ***T-16 Skyhopper Schematic **Stage 10: Prisons of Kessel ***TIE Fighter Schematic *Chapter III: The New Threat **Stage 11: Battle above Taloraan ***Blockade Runner Schematic **Stage 12: Escape from Fest ***Snowspeeder Schematic **Stage 13: Blockade at Chandrila ***Shuttle Schematic **Stage 14: Raid on Sullust ***AT-ST Schematic **Stage 15: Moff Seerdon's Revenge ***V-Wing Schematic *Chapter IV: Dark Empire **Stage 16: The Battle of Calamari ***TIE/D Fighter Schematic *Chapter V: The Hidden Stages and Secret Codes **Stage 17: Beggar's Canyon **Stage 18: The Death Star Trench Run **Stage 19: Battle of Hoth **Secret Codes **Hidden Ships ==Development== The credited publisher for ''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide'' was was [[M. Arakawa]] with [[Yoshio Tsuboike]] as associate publisher, [[Leslie Swan]] as editor in chief, [[Jessica Joffe Stein|Jessica Joffe]] as copy editor and [[Nancy Ramsey]] as production coordinator. [[Levi Buchanan]], [[Jason Leung]], [[Scott Pelland]] and [[Paul Shinoda]] were credited as "Writers/Editors" and [[Tim Casey]], [[Bryan Hartmann]], [[Jeff Storbo]], [[Lauren Tozer-Kilts]] and [[Pat Wells]] served as game consultants. [[Kim Logan]] served as art director, with [[Jonathan Dachs]] as designer and [[Jay Wergin]] as "Design/[[Wikipedia:Prepress|Prepress]] Supervisor". The guide's strategic layout was done by [[Jack Yushi|Jumpin' Jack Yushi]], [[Naoyuki Kayama]], [[Toshinori Sato]] and [[Yoshiyuki Oshino]] for [[Work Horse Co.|Work Horse Co., Ltd.]], and [[Yoshi Orimo]], [[Sonja Morris]] and [[Chris Inclenrock]] for [[V Design|V Design, Inc.]], with art by [[LucasArts]] and [[Lucasfilm]]. [[Jim Catechi]], [[Tim Garret]], [[Kathryn Miller]], [[Carol Walter]], [[Van Williams]] were credited as responsible for electronic prepress, with [[Todd Dyment]] as prepress assistant. The guide was printed in the [[Wikipedia:United States|United States]] and published by [[Nintendo|Nintendo of America Inc.]] The credits also give special thanks to "Joel Dreskin and the righteous Rebels from LucasArts and Lucasfilm, and Don James of Nintendo of America Inc." {{Book-stub}} ==Appearances== {{App |characters= *[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] *[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]
